name: Route of the Culatas - circular to Jarandilla de la Vera via the Carlos V Route
name_original: Ruta de las Culatas – circular a Jarandilla de la Vera por la Ruta de Carlos V
region_intro: Hiking trails around Aldeanueva De La Vera explore a landscape shaped by the Sierra de Gredos foothills and abundant water features. The region is characterized by numerous gorges, natural pools, and lush forests of chestnut and oak. This microclimate supports diverse vegetation, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails in Aldeanueva De La Vera The most popular hiking route is Route of the Culatas - circular to Jarandilla de la Vera via the Carlos V Route, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete, traversing historical paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fuente de los Chorros – Cuacos de Yuste loop from Jarandilla de la Vera, a moderate 9.1 miles (14.6 km) path. This route offers views of the local water features and surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Jarandilla de la Vera loop from Aldeanueva de la Vera, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around Aldeanueva De La Vera is defined by its gorges, natural pools, and the forested foothills of the Sierra de Gredos. The network of trails offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Aldeanueva De La Vera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 360 reviews. More than 2100 hikers have used komoot to explore Aldeanueva De La Vera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Aldeanueva de la Vera?
A: There are over 80 hiking trails available around Aldeanueva de la Vera, offering a wide range of options from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The komoot community has explored more than 80 routes in the area.

Q: Are there any hikes with natural pools or waterfalls in the area?
A: Yes, the region is renowned for its abundant water features. You can find stunning natural pools and waterfalls. Notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; within the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/874276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Trabuquete&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, which offer a popular and family-friendly route.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Aldeanueva de la Vera?
A: The terrain around Aldeanueva de la Vera is diverse, ranging from lush forests of chestnut and oak to the rugged foothills of the Sierra de Gredos. You'll encounter paths along gorges, beside natural pools, and through scrubland, providing varied landscapes and elevation changes.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Aldeanueva de la Vera?
A: Yes, there are several family-friendly hiking options. The route to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; is particularly popular with families due to its accessible paths and multiple viewpoints. Many of the easier trails (over 25 available) are also suitable for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Aldeanueva de la Vera region. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in nature reserves or areas with livestock, and to check specific regulations for protected areas like the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990331563/route-of-the-culatas-circular-to-jarandilla-de-la-vera-via-the-carlos-v-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Culatas - circular to Jarandilla de la Vera via the Carlos V Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.5-mile loop.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I discover while hiking?
A: The region offers several historical points of interest. You can explore the renowned Route of Carlos V, which traverses the area. Additionally, a route to the Cerro de los Tres Picos leads to the vestiges of a 4000-year-old Celtic settlement, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6084589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Yuste&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical site nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Aldeanueva de la Vera?
A: Thanks to its microclimate, Aldeanueva de la Vera's hills remain green almost year-round. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant for hiking, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer offers refreshing dips in the natural pools, while winter can provide a different, quieter experience, especially in the lower elevations.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads, or is parking necessary?
A: While some trailheads might be accessible by local bus services, having a car generally offers more flexibility to reach various starting points for hikes around Aldeanueva de la Vera. Parking is usually available near popular trailheads and villages.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Aldeanueva de la Vera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 360 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural landscapes, the refreshing natural pools, and the diverse terrain that offers something for every ability level.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, while there are many moderate and easy trails, the region also offers more challenging hikes, particularly those venturing higher into the Sierra de Gredos foothills. These routes often feature greater elevation gains and more rugged paths, with 8 difficult routes available for experienced trekkers.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views of the Sierra de Gredos?
A: Many trails in the region offer spectacular panoramic views of the Sierra de Gredos, especially those that ascend to higher elevations. The Mirador Chorrero de la Virgen provides an excellent vantage point to view the Manto de la Virgen waterfall and the surrounding mountains.

Q: What are some notable gorges to explore in the region?
A: The region is famous for its gorges. The Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve is home to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones gorge&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable gorges include the Garganta de San Gregorio, featuring a medieval bridge, and the Gualtamino Gorge, also known as the Devil's Waterfall, near Villanueva de la Vera.

Q: Are there any easy walks suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, Aldeanueva de la Vera offers plenty of easy walks suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. There are 27 easy routes available, often following gentler paths through forests or along riverbanks, perfect for enjoying the natural beauty without significant effort.
tips: In the center of the orchard of La Vera and El Tiétar, between water gorges, natural pools, chestnut and oak forests, and natural landscapes that reaffirm the richness of the historical and monumental environment, the Parador de Jarandilla de la Vera watches the centuries go by , castle-palace where Emperor Charles V stayed for months.

The medieval or Roman bridge, as the Jarandillanos usually call it, is 35 meters long, has a huge eye with a semicircular arch and a smaller arch on one side that serves as a spillway when the Jaranda Gorge goes down with a lot of water, It sits on the enormous granite masses that are in the riverbed and has a half-meter petril with masonry ashlars.

waypoints.name: Parador de Jarandilla de la Vera
Bridge of Jarandilla de la Vera
